chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitutional Petition No.D-983 of 2024. [Mehdi Hassan and others vs. Province of Sindh and others] Connected with Constitutional Petition No.D-1787 of 2024. [Nadeem Ahmed Akhund and others vs. Province of Sindh and others] Constitutional Petition No.D-1376 of 2024. [Allah Bux and others vs. Province of Sindh and others] Constitutional Petition No.D-1377 of 2024. [Saddam Hussain and others vs. Province of Sindh and others] Constitutional Petition No.D-1718 of 2024. [Mehtab Ali and others vs. Province of Sindh and others] Constitutional Petition No.D-1832 of 2024. [Imdad Ali and others vs. Province of Sindh and others] Constitutional Petition No.D-2095 of 2024. [Anwar Ali and others vs. Province of Sindh and others] Constitutional Petition No.D-1218 of 2025. [Mst. Fozia and others vs. Province of Sindh and others] *************************** Before:- Mr. Justice Amjad Ali Bohio, Mr. Justice Ali Haider ‘Ada’. For hearing of C.M.A No.2797/2026 (Contempt application). For hearing of C.M.A No.2803/2026 (Contempt application). For hearing of C.M.A No.1308/2026 (Contempt application). For hearing of C.M.A No.1262/2026 (Contempt application). For hearing of C.M.A No.1297/2026 (Contempt application). For hearing of C.M.A No.1392/2026 (Contempt application). For hearing of C.M.A No.1300/2026 (Contempt application). For hearing of C.M.A No.1303/2026 (Contempt application). 29.04.2026. M/s. Mehfooz Ahmed Awan, Farhan Ali Shaikh and Badaruddin Memon, advocates for the Petitioners. Mr. Ali Raza Balouch, Additional Advocate General along with Tanveer Ahmed, ADC-I, Sukkur, Irshad Ahmed, Deputy Director, Water Management Department, Mir Javed Ali, District Population Welfare Officer, Sukkur, Sheeraz Ali Bhayo, AXEN Spur Sub-Division, Sukkur on behalf of XEN

Mr. Ali Raza Balouch, Additional Advocate General along with Tanveer Ahmed, ADC-I, Sukkur, Irshad Ahmed, Deputy Director, Water Management Department, Mir Javed Ali, District Population Welfare Officer, Sukkur, Sheeraz Ali Bhayo, AXEN Spur Sub-Division, Sukkur on behalf of XEN 2 Barrage Sukkur, Wajahat Haider Janwari, XEN, District Highways Works and Services Department, Raza H. Qureshi, Deputy Director, Focal Person, Court Matters GMMMC Hospital, Sukkur, Ahsanullah Simiar, Additional Director, Agriculture Extension, Sukkur..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. Ali Haider ‘Ada’ J.- By this single order, the proceedings in all the above-captioned constitutional petitions as well as connected contempt applications are being disposed of. The controversy in all these petitions relates to recruitment against posts in BPS-01 to BPS-04, for which various departments invited applications through walk-in interviews and advertisements. The petitioners, being aggrieved by non-consideration or delay in finalization of their cases, had approached this Court. In compliance of the directions issued by this Court, the Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur, in his capacity as head of the District Recruitment Committee (DRC), has submitted comprehensive reports. The concerned departments, namely the Agricultural Extension Department, Farm Water Management Department, Barrage Division Sukkur, Population Welfare Department, Highway Division Sukkur, and Ghulam Muhammad Mahar Medical College, have also placed on record relevant documents and proceedings of the DRC, including minutes of meetings and recommendations. In Constitutional Petition No. D-983 of 2024 and C.P. No. D- 1787 of 2024, pertaining to the Agricultural Extension Department, this Court had earlier passed an order dated 17.12.2025. Due to subsequent non-compliance, contempt applications bearing CMA Nos. 2797 and 2803 of 2026 were filed. The department has now submitted its compliance report along with minutes of the DRC, reflecting that the petitioners have been declared successful and recommended for appointment.

Due to subsequent non-compliance, contempt applications bearing CMA Nos. 2797 and 2803 of 2026 were filed. The department has now submitted its compliance report along with minutes of the DRC, reflecting that the petitioners have been declared successful and recommended for appointment. In view thereof, learned counsel for 3 the petitioners has expressed satisfaction; accordingly, the said contempt applications are disposed of. Similarly, in Constitutional Petition No. D-1376 of 2024 (Population Welfare Department), the compliance report reflects that the petitioners have been duly recommended for appointment. Learned counsel for the petitioners, being satisfied, does not press CMA No. 1308 of 2026, which is accordingly disposed of. In Constitutional Petition No. D-1377 of 2024 (Farm Water Management Department), the DRC has recommended the cases of the petitioners and the issuance of appointment letters has been affirmed. CMA No. 1262 of 2026 is accordingly disposed of as not pressed. In Constitutional Petition No. D-1718 of 2024 (Population Welfare Department), the compliance report indicates that the petitioners have been recommended for appointment to BPS-01 to BPS-04 posts. Learned counsel, being satisfied, does not press CMA No. 1297 of 2026, which is disposed of. In Constitutional Petition No. D-1832 of 2024 (Highway Division Sukkur), the Executive Engineer has appeared and submitted a report confirming that the petitioners’ cases have been recommended and verified by the Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur. Learned counsel for the petitioners, being satisfied, does not press CMA No. 1392 of 2026, which is disposed of. In Constitutional Petition No. D-2095 of 2024 and C.P. No. D-1218 of 2025 (relating to Ghulam Muhammad Mahar Medical College), the compliance report reflects that the petitioners have been recommended for appointment. The Medical Superintendent has also affirmed the same. Learned counsel for the petitioners, being satisfied, does not press CMA Nos. 1300 and 1303 of 2026, which are accordingly disposed of.

The Medical Superintendent has also affirmed the same. Learned counsel for the petitioners, being satisfied, does not press CMA Nos. 1300 and 1303 of 2026, which are accordingly disposed of. 4 In view of the above, it is evident that compliance has substantially been made in all the above petitions, and the cases of the petitioners have been duly recommended by the District Recruitment Committee, comprising the Deputy Commissioner, heads of the respective departments, and representatives of the S&GAD. Accordingly, while disposing of these petitions and the connected contempt applications, the respondents, particularly the Deputy Commissioner, Sukkur, and the concerned departmental authorities, are directed to issue appointment letters to the petitioners against posts in BPS-01 to BPS-04, strictly in accordance with the recommendations of the DRC, within a period of fifteen days from the date of receipt of this order, positively. All pending applications, including contempt applications, stand disposed of in the above terms. Order accordingly. The office is directed to place signed copies of this order on the record of all connected petitions. J U D G E J U D G E
